body:
## Problem

SpeakableSpecification is implemented on drummer profile pages (packages/frontend/utils/speakableSchema.js) but NOT on article pages.

62 articles (/articles/<slug>) have Article JSON-LD (per #1064/#777) but zero SpeakableSpecification. The lead paragraph of every drum setup article ('X uses Y drums and Z cymbals') is exactly the kind of short, factual, quotable sentence that voice assistants (Google Assistant, Siri) read aloud in response to 'What drum kit does X use?'

This is an LLM/voice citation surface gap: the most direct answer to our primary search intent sits in article lead paragraphs, currently invisible to voice search.

## Fix

Already shipped in PR #1574. `speakableSchema: true` is set on both the `ARTICLE_METADATA` return block (line ~848) and the `ALBUM_ARTICLES` return block (line ~935) in `api/meta/[...path].js`. The `generateSpeakableSchema()` function at line ~2039 emits the SpeakableSpecification JSON-LD with `cssSelector: ['h1', '.article-lead', '.key-fact']`. The function is called in `generateMetaHtml()` and included in every SSR response.

## Done

- [x] `speakableSchema: true` on ARTICLE_METADATA block (covers hand-tuned articles, line 848)
- [x] `speakableSchema: true` on ALBUM_ARTICLES HowTo block (line 900)
- [x] `speakableSchema: true` on ALBUM_ARTICLES block (covers all 62 album articles, line 935)
- [x] `generateSpeakableSchema()` emits SpeakableSpecification with correct cssSelector
- [x] Included in `generateMetaHtml()` output — every article SSR response contains the block
- [x] No regression on Article JSON-LD or BreadcrumbList schema
title: SEO: SpeakableSpecification schema on article pages — voice search eligibility (62 articles)
